typing.text: how should I design the Stage 1A cursor pre-summarizer? what model should I use and what should the prompt look like?
ok so for Stage 1B the timeline builder — should I merge events by timestamp or by app? what gap threshold makes sense?

user_turns: I want to build a two-stage summarizer pipeline. Stage 1A should pre-summarize each Cursor session using a cheap haiku model. What's the best prompt structure?

Good. Now for Stage 1B — the timeline builder — I want to merge ActivityWatch raw events with typing entries. The key challenge is matching typing entries to AW segments by timestamp AND bundle_id. How should I handle the bundle_id to app-name mapping?
The _BUNDLE_TO_AW_APP dict approach makes sense. One concern: what if AW isn't running and raw_events is empty? Should the timeline just have synthetic Cursor segments?

Perfect. Let's also add the token budget enforcement in build_prompt — if the timeline exceeds max_timeline_tokens, drop charcount segments first, then truncate typing lists. Implement this in _render_timeline_section.

meetings.title: Architecture Review — Daily Agent
meetings.summary: Reviewed the two-stage summarizer design with the team. Key decisions: (1) Use claude-haiku for Stage 1A Cursor pre-summarizer — faster and cheaper for mechanical condensing. (2) Timeline builder merges same-app events within 60-second gaps, drops segments under 3 minutes. (3) Token budget enforcement drops charcount-only segments first, then truncates typing lists. Action item: implement and test with real ActivityWatch data this week.

context.general: Building a personal daily productivity agent that runs locally on macOS. The agent collects computer activity (typing logs, ActivityWatch, Cursor sessions, Notion meetings), synthesizes a daily summary via Claude API, and delivers it to Telegram at 8 PM. Currently in active build phase — completing the pipeline modules.
